solve_notes: Added `checkNotNull(parts)` as the second statement of the base `Joiner.appendTo(A appendable, Iterator<?> parts)` method at line 104 in Joiner.java. The skipNulls() override at line 309 already checks both appendable and parts; the base method was only checking appendable.
task_text:
Fix Joiner.appendTo(Appendable, Iterator) missing null-check for the parts iterator

The base `Joiner.appendTo(A appendable, Iterator<?> parts)` method does
not call `checkNotNull(parts)` before iterating. Passing a `null`
iterator throws `NullPointerException` at `parts.hasNext()` deep inside
the method with a confusing stack trace. The inner class returned by
`skipNulls()` already overrides this method and does call
`checkNotNull(parts, "parts")`. Fix the base `Joiner.appendTo(A,
Iterator)` method in `Joiner.java` to add `checkNotNull(parts)` as its
first statement, consistent with the `skipNulls()` variant.

The skipNulls override is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent would search for the reference implementation to understand the expected style (message argument or not, order of checks) and without it might choose a slightly different form and then revise.
